analyst.description: A synthesized analyst persona based on in-depth interviews with three domain experts.

scenario.domainProblem: Misinformation and anomalous retweeting behavior circulated widely during the 2012 Hurricane Sandy event.
scenario.analysisGoal: Explore Twitter threads, identify anomalous rumor-related conversations, compare related rumor threads, and examine model mechanisms behind anomaly scores and hidden states.
scenario.datasetContext: Top 100 abnormal retweeting threads from the Hurricane Sandy Twitter dataset ranked by anomaly score.
scenario.stakes: Identifying rumor-spreading behavior in crisis communication can support understanding of misinformation diffusion.
scenario.initialQuestion: Which anomalous retweeting threads deserve investigation, how are key rumor threads related, and what model patterns help explain their anomaly scores?

questions.questionText: Which retweeting threads in the Hurricane Sandy dataset appear anomalous enough to merit misinformation investigation?
questions.description: The analyst looks for suspicious high-anomaly rumor-related threads using both bottom-up and top-down exploration.

quotes: identifying anomalous conversational threads
This was proven to be a rumor afterwards
Both have been indicated as rumors later
evidence.reasoning: The case explicitly reports multiple suspicious threads that were later verified as rumors.

questions.questionText: How are prominent rumor-related threads related in topics, user overlap, and social connections?
questions.description: The analyst compares the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier topic with power outage and shark rumor threads and inspects overlapping users and links.

quotes: the analyst wants to see if there is any relations between the participating users in the two "rumor" topic threads
there are many overlaps, and a few users have close social network relationships
evidence.reasoning: The tool reveals overlap and possible social proximity, but the narrative does not fully resolve broader causal relationships.

questions.questionText: What hidden-state and feature patterns help explain why some threads receive higher anomaly scores than others?
questions.description: The analyst compares timeline shapes, hidden-state patterns, and feature heatmaps across threads with different anomaly levels.

quotes: the analyst wants to thoroughly understand the model mechanisms
future studies are needed to better understand the OCCRF model
threads with higher anomaly scores tend to have larger values in features measuring the activities of users
evidence.reasoning: The paper reports meaningful model-related observations but explicitly leaves the mechanism only partly resolved.

episodes.localGoal: Start from anomalous individual threads, inspect one suspicious topic in depth, and confirm it as a rumor example.
episodes.strategyText: Begin from visually suspicious anomalous leaves, then trace into a parent topic and its children to understand the rumor thread.

steps.rawNarrative: The analyst restricts attention to leaf threads in the MDS view, uses glyph color and position to pick anomalous candidates, and imports selected threads into the threads view.
steps.intentText: Find suspicious individual threads worth deeper inspection.
targetDataDescription: Leaf retweeting threads among the top 100 abnormal threads
A selected thread with anomaly score 0.97

observations.description: A leaf thread with very high anomaly score and unusual content stands out among the selected timelines.
targetDataDescription: A thread quoting the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier photo

quotes: one with very high anomaly score (0.97) and interesting content catches her eye
evidence.reasoning: The narrative explicitly says the analyst notices one striking high-score thread.

producedInsights.description: The Tomb of the Unknown Soldier thread is selected as a candidate anomalous rumor topic for deeper drill-down.

evidence.reasoning: The selected high-score thread becomes the focus of the next drill-down step.

decision.description: Drill into the suspicious Tomb of the Unknown Soldier topic and examine its broader thread context.

evidence.reasoning: The next sentences explicitly describe tracing the thread to its parent topic.

steps.outcomeSummary: A specific suspicious leaf thread is prioritized for topic-level inspection.

quotes: she first choses to show only the leaf threads in the MDS view
evidence.reasoning: This step is directly narrated in the bottom-up passage.

steps.rawNarrative: The analyst traces the suspicious thread to its parent topic in the cluster view, inspects thread details and volume patterns, expands all child threads, and reads a bizarre rumor tweet from a highly abnormal child thread.
steps.intentText: Determine whether the suspicious topic and its child threads look like misinformation.
targetDataDescription: Parent thread about the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier picture
Child rumor thread retweeted by 63 users within 3 hours

observations.description: The parent thread lasts about 14.5 hours with multiple peaks, and user anomaly scores generally transition from low to high over time.
targetDataDescription: Parent topic thread about the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier picture

quotes: lasting about 14.5 hours and involving a total of 407 users
the user anomaly scores generally transit from low to high along time
evidence.reasoning: The timeline view is explicitly used to characterize the topic thread's temporal pattern.

observations.description: A child thread contains many highly abnormal users and includes a bizarre claim about Obama and the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier.
targetDataDescription: A child thread under the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier topic

quotes: In one child thread that is full of "purple" abnormal users
"Obama tells marrines they don't have to guard the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier. They refuse."
evidence.reasoning: The narrative explicitly links the thread's abnormal coloring with an implausible rumor tweet.

producedInsights.description: The Tomb of the Unknown Soldier topic contains at least one clearly rumor-like anomalous child thread.

quotes: This was proven to be a rumor afterwards
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly confirms the selected child thread as a rumor example.

steps.outcomeSummary: The analyst confirms one anomalous rumor topic by combining structural drill-down, detailed metadata, and raw tweet inspection.

evidence.reasoning: The bottom-up paragraph explicitly ends with a rumor-confirming child thread.

episodes.episodeOutcomeSummary: Bottom-up exploration isolates the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier topic as a concrete anomalous rumor example and shows how its children expose suspicious content.

evidence.reasoning: Order 110 defines this phase and its local outcome.

episodes.localGoal: Use the hierarchy and MDS overview to find another anomalous topic, decompose it into subtopics, and interpret its bursts.
episodes.strategyText: Manipulate the hierarchy at a higher level, then unfold and compare subtopics to explain a suspicious thread's temporal structure.

steps.rawNarrative: The analyst collapses the hierarchy to reduce clutter, identifies a relatively high-anomaly thread, reads topical cues from the tooltip, and unfolds its timeline.
steps.intentText: Find another promising anomalous topic from the overview and inspect its overall temporal pattern.
targetDataDescription: A relatively high-anomaly parent thread with keywords including power plant, home, and swim

observations.description: Tooltip metadata suggests an interesting topic through keywords such as power plant, home, and swim.

quotes: from the tooltip she can see a few interesting keywords of the tweets, such as "power plant", "home" and "swim"
evidence.reasoning: The tooltip directly supplies the topical cues used for choosing the thread.

observations.description: The selected thread has mostly high-anomaly users and a three-peak volume pattern with a large burst at the end.

quotes: most of the users have scores above 0.75
the user volume seems to form three peaks, with a large burst in the end
evidence.reasoning: The timeline inspection explicitly reveals both anomaly distribution and burst structure.

decision.description: Expand the selected thread to inspect whether its subthreads explain the burst structure.

evidence.reasoning: The next part of the paragraph explicitly moves to expanding the node into child threads.

steps.outcomeSummary: A second suspicious topic is selected and characterized as temporally bursty with mostly high-anomaly users.

evidence.reasoning: The top-down paragraph explicitly reports the chosen thread and its timeline pattern.

steps.rawNarrative: The analyst expands the selected node into three child threads, compares them via glyph encodings and MDS proximity, and attributes the final burst to two short rumor-related child threads.
steps.intentText: Explain the burst structure by decomposing the thread into subtopics and comparing them.
targetDataDescription: Three child threads under the selected high-anomaly parent topic
Two short rumor-related child threads about power outage and a shark swimming down the street

observations.description: Two child threads are shorter in duration, one is especially anomalous, and the pair is closer together in MDS space than the third child thread.

quotes: two of them last very little time, including one with a significantly higher anomaly score
these two threads appear much closer in the MDS view
evidence.reasoning: The cluster and MDS views jointly support the comparison among child subtopics.

observations.description: The final burst in the parent timeline is caused by the two shorter rumor-related child threads.

quotes: the analyst further observes that the final burst is caused by the two shorter threads
evidence.reasoning: The narrative explicitly connects the child threads to the burst pattern in the timeline.

producedInsights.description: The selected parent thread decomposes into three subtopics, with two shorter rumor-related child threads accounting for the late burst.

evidence.reasoning: The subtopic decomposition and burst explanation are explicit in the text.

steps.outcomeSummary: Top-down exploration identifies two additional rumor-related threads and explains how they generate the parent thread's final burst.

evidence.reasoning: The paragraph explicitly ties the child rumors to the burst and names their topics.

episodes.episodeOutcomeSummary: Top-down overview analysis finds another suspicious parent thread, decomposes it into rumor-bearing subtopics, and relates those subtopics to temporal bursts.

evidence.reasoning: Order 111 explicitly defines this top-down analytic phase.

episodes.localGoal: Compare two rumor-related threads to inspect shared users and social links.
episodes.strategyText: Juxtapose two previously identified rumor threads and inspect overlap plus user-link overlays to judge whether they may be socially related.

steps.rawNarrative: The analyst opens the two rumor-related threads together, enables duplicated-user highlighting and user-connection overlays, and inspects overlap and close relationships among participants.
steps.intentText: Assess whether two rumor threads share users or network relationships worth further investigation.
targetDataDescription: The Tomb of the Unknown Soldier rumor thread
A second rumor-related thread from the top-down exploration

observations.description: The two rumor threads share many users, and a few of those users have close social network relationships.

quotes: there are many overlaps, and a few users have close social network relationships
evidence.reasoning: The overlap and connection overlays directly support this observation.

producedInsights.description: The two rumor-related threads are linked by overlapping participants and some close user connections, suggesting potentially related rumor-spreading behavior.

evidence.reasoning: The analyst explicitly interprets the overlap as evidence meriting further investigation.

decision.description: Flag the overlapping users as candidates for deeper rumor-spreading investigation.

quotes: those users might merit further investigation into their rumor-spreading behavior
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly presents a next investigative commitment.

steps.outcomeSummary: Cross-thread comparison reveals overlapping and socially connected participants across the two rumor topics.

evidence.reasoning: The comparison block explicitly ends with this takeaway.

episodes.episodeOutcomeSummary: Comparative inspection suggests that two rumor threads share user participation patterns and possible social ties worth follow-up analysis.

evidence.reasoning: The connection-and-comparison subsection is explicit in order 111.

episodes.localGoal: Compare threads across anomaly levels and inspect hidden-state and feature patterns that may explain model behavior.
episodes.strategyText: Build a contrast set across anomaly levels, compare temporal patterns, then inspect hidden-state projections and feature heatmaps to form partial explanations.

steps.rawNarrative: The analyst samples six threads across high, medium, and low anomaly levels, aligns them in time, and compares their timeline shapes and anomaly patterns.
steps.intentText: Look for temporal patterns that distinguish more anomalous threads from less anomalous ones.
targetDataDescription: Six threads spanning high, medium, and low anomaly-score levels

observations.description: Threads with short-time bursts or long tails tend to receive larger anomaly scores.

quotes: threads that have short-time bursts or long tails are likely to be assigned larger anomaly scores
evidence.reasoning: The compared aligned timelines directly support this model-related temporal observation.

observations.description: User anomaly scores alone do not deterministically explain thread abnormality because some threads disagree between thread-level and user-level anomaly patterns.

quotes: user anomaly scores... do not have determinative effects alone on the abnormality of threads
evidence.reasoning: The narrative explicitly states a mismatch between thread and user anomaly patterns.

producedInsights.description: Temporal comparison reveals useful tendencies, but those tendencies are insufficient to fully explain the anomaly model.

quotes: future studies are needed to better understand the OCCRF model
evidence.reasoning: The paragraph explicitly frames the explanation as incomplete.

decision.description: Inspect hidden states and feature values to search for deeper explanatory structure beyond raw anomaly scores.

evidence.reasoning: The next two paragraphs explicitly move from timeline comparison to hidden states and features.

steps.outcomeSummary: Temporal comparison yields partial model insights but leaves the anomaly mechanism unresolved.

evidence.reasoning: Order 112 directly states both the observations and the remaining uncertainty.

steps.rawNarrative: The analyst compares hidden-state color patterns across threads, inspects state projections in the states view, and checks feature heatmaps to find attributes associated with higher anomaly scores.
steps.intentText: Use hidden states and feature values to form a deeper partial explanation of anomaly behavior.
targetDataDescription: Feature heatmaps for a couple of threads

observations.description: Highly anomalous threads mostly remain in the pink state, whereas lower-anomaly threads show more state variation.

quotes: highly anomalous retweeting threads mostly remain in the "pink" state
those with lower anomaly scores are likely to have more variations of state colors
evidence.reasoning: The paper directly contrasts state patterns across higher- and lower-anomaly threads.

observations.description: Threads with higher anomaly scores tend to show larger values for activity-related features such as status count, followers count, and out degree.

quotes: threads with higher anomaly scores tend to have larger values in features measuring the activities of users
evidence.reasoning: The feature view explicitly supports this activity-feature comparison.

producedInsights.description: Persistent pink hidden states and elevated activity-related feature values provide a partial explanation for why some rumor-related threads look more anomalous.

evidence.reasoning: The final model-understanding observations combine state and feature evidence into a partial explanation.

steps.outcomeSummary: State and feature inspection add a deeper but still incomplete explanation of anomaly patterns across rumor-related threads.

evidence.reasoning: Orders 113-114 explicitly report these deeper insights while the prior paragraph keeps the question only partially resolved.

episodes.episodeOutcomeSummary: The analyst forms a partial explanation of model behavior by comparing thread shapes, hidden-state persistence, and activity-related features, but the mechanism is not fully resolved.

evidence.reasoning: The final three paragraphs explicitly define this model-understanding phase and its limits.

finalInsights.description: FluxFlow helps surface multiple rumor-related anomalous threads during the Hurricane Sandy event, including the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier, power outage, and shark rumor topics.

evidence.reasoning: The case explicitly identifies several anomalous rumor topics across the bottom-up and top-down episodes.

finalInsights.description: Comparison across rumor-related threads reveals participant overlap and some close social connections that may indicate related rumor-spreading behavior.

evidence.reasoning: The connection-and-comparison block explicitly reports this relationship-oriented finding.

finalInsights.description: Persistent pink hidden states and elevated activity-related features partially explain high anomaly scores, but the full OCCRF mechanism remains unresolved.

evidence.reasoning: The final model-understanding sequence supports a partial explanatory claim and also notes open uncertainty.

finalDecisions.description: Users overlapping across rumor threads should be investigated further for rumor-spreading behavior.

quotes: those users might merit further investigation into their rumor-spreading behavior
evidence.reasoning: The paper explicitly states this follow-up implication.

finalOutcome.claimedSystemValue: FluxFlow supports complementary bottom-up and top-down exploration, cross-thread comparison, and partial explanation of the anomaly model in a misinformation-focused social-media case study.

evidence.reasoning: The case narrative demonstrates these different forms of support across the full walkthrough.

caseStudies.overallStrategySummary: The case alternates between bottom-up anomaly triage and top-down structural exploration, then compares rumor threads directly and finally inspects hidden-state and feature evidence to partially explain model behavior.
caseStudies.caseNarrativeSummary: In the Hurricane Sandy case study, a synthesized analyst uses FluxFlow to surface suspicious rumor-related retweeting threads, decomposes and compares those threads across hierarchy and time, inspects overlap among rumor participants, and then studies hidden states and feature values to form a partial explanation of why some threads appear more anomalous.

evidence.reasoning: The full case-study block supports the end-to-end narrative and episode segmentation.

paperLevelUsageClaims: The paper demonstrates FluxFlow through a synthesized case-study walkthrough grounded in expert interviews.
FluxFlow supports complementary bottom-up and top-down analysis, cross-thread comparison, and model-understanding activities in the Hurricane Sandy misinformation setting.

evidence.reasoning: Order 105 frames the existence of one analysis use case, and orders 109-114 plus the case-study figures provide the actual walkthrough evidence.
